metadata.title: FFT vs Naive Convolution: A 1000× Speedup from Math

beats.narration_text: Bula. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of fft vs naive convolution: a 1000× speedup from math. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of fft vs naive convolution: a 1000× speedup from math
output: ✓ FFT vs Naive Convolution: A 1000× Speedup from Math

beats.narration_text: FFT vs Naive Convolution: A 1000× Speedup from Math is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Same mathematical result. Radically different cost. The FFT exploits roots of unity to reduce O(n²) multiplications to O(n log n).. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: FFT vs Naive Convolution: A 1000× Speedup from Math

beats.narration_text: Same mathematical result. Radically different cost. The FFT exploits roots of unity to reduce O(n²) multiplications to O(n log n).. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of fft vs naive convolution: a 1000× speedup from math. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
